headline: Verizon (VZ): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q1 Earnings?
summary: Verizon trades at $44.98 and has moved in lockstep with the market. Its shares have returned 7.9% over the last six months while the S&P 500 has gained 10%.

headline: Forget Wall Street’s Ultimate Telecom Value Trap and Buy the Real Free Cash Flow King
summary: Income investors have piled into Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) this year, sending the stock up 21.4% year to date as the crowd chases a 5.78% dividend yield and the company’s old “premium network” reputation. But here’s what you should actually be watching. Verizon is the textbook telecom value trap. The stock now carries a $199.3 billion ... Forget Wall Street’s Ultimate Telecom Value Trap and Buy the Real Free Cash Flow King

headline: Do Verizon (VZ) Debt Tenders and Dividend Steadiness Hint at a Shifting Capital Priorities Playbook?
summary: In early June 2026, Verizon Communications’ board declared a quarterly dividend of US$0.7075 per share and advanced a complex mix of cash tender and exchange offers across about US$4.30 billion of subsidiary and parent notes, extending key participation deadlines to June 16, 2026. These moves come as Verizon manages heavier regulatory scrutiny following a Supreme Court loss and mounting broadband competition from SpaceX’s Starlink, putting its capital structure and cash-return priorities...

headline: Elon Musk’s $1.8 Trillion SpaceX IPO Is Too Big to Succeed
summary: The  SpaceX  initial public offering is one of a kind; the investment opportunity is not.  SpaceX’s IPO is arguably the biggest capital-markets event ever, and unique.  SpaceX’s singularity will continue when it starts trading, with the company added to the  just 15 trading days after the offering, requiring passive buying of 10% to 15% of the shares outstanding, and a massive amount of retail participation in the IPO.

headline: Verizon CEO: AI will take over ‘a large percentage’ of customer service
summary: AI “will dramatically improve our ability to satisfy customers,” Dan Schulman said. The carrier says it’s already had success with agents replacing some of its customer service representatives.

headline: Verizon (VZ) Stock Trades Down, Here Is Why
summary: Shares of telecommunications giant Verizon (NYSE:VZ) fell 3.9% in the afternoon session after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled against the company, backing the Federal Communications Commission's (FCC) authority to fine it for mishandling customer location data.
